checks.a_analysis: The output contains only information present in the source document, with the exception of the metadata title which is an expected AI-generated addition for accessibility. No hallucinated body text, invented sections, or fabricated conclusions were found.

All phone numbers (828-258-3939, 877-626-3198), addresses, and numeric passcodes (8186681, 445085031) present in the output correctly match the source document. No fabricated contact information was detected.

All primary sections (Header, Meeting Details, Agenda) and the single page of the source document are represented in the output. While a critical piece of information (the WebEx URL) is missing, it constitutes an omission within a section rather than a missing section in its entirety.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The WebEx join URL (http://www.mymeetings.com/nc/join.php?sigKey=mymeetings&i=445085031&p=IndigoSnake&t=c) is entirely missing from the output. This is a critical piece of information for accessing the remote meeting described in the document. Its absence is evidenced by the missing element index 6 in the reading order sequence (skipping from 5 to 7). Within the short WebEx subsection (consisting of a header, URL, meeting number, and passcode), this omission represents 25% of the required information.
checks.b_evidence_output: [The URL is missing from the output between reading_order 5 and 7]

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: All text content present in the output faithfully represents the source document's wording and meaning. Minor reformatting, such as merging multiple lines (e.g., location and phone number or meeting number and passcode) into single paragraph elements, is acceptable and does not alter the information provided.

checks.a_analysis: Each content block from the source (headings, address, remote access info, and agenda items) appears only once in the output. There is no evidence of repeated paragraphs or sections.
The heading hierarchy is logical. The document title is an H1, and the 'Agenda:' section is correctly identified as an H2. There are no skipped levels or nesting errors.
The source document contains no tables.

The output maintains the logical top-to-bottom flow of the source document. Although there is a gap in the element numbering (skipping 6), the elements present (1-5, 7-9) follow the correct sequence of the original text.
The agenda items, which appear as a series of timestamped lines in the source, have been correctly grouped into a structured 'list' type in the JSON output. The nesting and itemization are accurate.

The WebEx join URL present in the source document is entirely missing from the JSON output. This is evident by the gap in 'reading_order' indices (between 5 and 7) where the URL should have been located.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 1, after WebEx: paragraph, before Meeting Number paragraph.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The metadata (Date, Location, Remote Access, WebEx) is presented at the top, followed by the agenda list. The conversion from a plain text list in the PDF to a bulleted list in the HTML is a standard responsive design choice that preserves the visual hierarchy and meaning. The information flow remains identical.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table rendering issues are possible.
The source PDF is a text-only document with no logos, photos, or diagrams. No images are missing from the output.

There is no visual duplication of body content. The document title 'Federal Interagency Committee' appearing in the top navigation/header area as well as the main body heading is an expected behavior of the accessibility engine as defined in the instructions.
